user_message: Just came out of escalation triage with Evergreen Solutions. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 17:49 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 16:22 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 12:15 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-K trap (Consolidated view when separate-thread view would be more useful); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-K failure mode (Consolidated view when separate-thread view would be more useful)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-K -- Consolidated view when separate-thread view would be more useful
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
